children.heading: Advanced battery
children.label: 1 Advanced battery
children.text: The term “advanced battery” means a battery that consists of a battery cell that can be integrated into a module, pack, or system to be used in energy storage applications, including electric vehicles and the electric grid.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “advanced battery component” means a component of an advanced battery.

children.heading: Inclusions
children.label: B Inclusions
children.text: The term “advanced battery component” includes materials, enhancements, enclosures, anodes, cathodes, electrolytes, cells, and other associated technologies that comprise an advanced battery.

children.heading: Battery material
children.label: 3 Battery material
children.text: The term “battery material” means the raw and processed form of a mineral, metal, chemical, or other material used in an advanced battery component.

children.heading: Eligible entity
children.label: 4 Eligible entity
children.text: The term “eligible entity” means an entity described in any of paragraphs (1) through (5) of .section 16353(b) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: designated as a foreign terrorist organization by the Secretary of State under ;section 1189(a) of title 8

children.label: B
children.text: included on the list of specially designated nationals and blocked persons maintained by the Office of Foreign Assets Control of the Department of the Treasury (commonly known as the “SDN list”);

children.label: C
children.text: owned by, controlled by, or subject to the jurisdiction or direction of a government of a foreign country that is a covered nation (as defined in section 2533c(d) of title 10);1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: i
children.text: chapter 37 of title 18 (commonly known as the “Espionage Act”);

children.label: ii
children.text: section 951 or 1030 of title 18;

children.label: iii
children.text: chapter 90 of title 18 (commonly known as the “Economic Espionage Act of 1996”);

children.label: iv
children.text: the Arms Export Control Act ( et seq.);22 U.S.C. 2751

children.label: v
children.text: section 224, 225, 226, 227, or 236 of the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 (, 2275, 2276, 2277, and 2284);42 U.S.C. 22742So in original. Probably should be “or”.2

children.label: vi
children.text: the Export Control Reform Act of 2018 ( et seq.); or50 U.S.C. 4801

children.label: vii
children.text: the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( et seq.); or50 U.S.C. 1701

children.label: E
children.text: determined by the Secretary, in consultation with the Secretary of Defense and the Director of National Intelligence, to be engaged in unauthorized conduct that is detrimental to the national security or foreign policy of the United States.

children.heading: Manufacturing
children.label: 6 Manufacturing
children.text: The term “manufacturing”, with respect to an advanced battery and an advanced battery component, means the industrial and chemical steps taken to produce that advanced battery or advanced battery component, respectively.

children.heading: Processing
children.label: 7 Processing
children.text: The term “processing”, with respect to battery material, means the refining of materials, including the treating, baking, and coating processes used to convert raw products into constituent materials employed directly in advanced battery manufacturing.

children.heading: Recycling
children.label: 8 Recycling
children.text: The term “recycling” means the recovery of materials from advanced batteries to be reused in similar applications, including the extracting, processing, and recoating of battery materials and advanced battery components.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall establish within the Office of Fossil Energy a program, to be known as the “Battery Material Processing Grant Program” (referred to in this subsection as the “program”), under which the Secretary shall award grants in accordance with this subsection.November 15, 2021

children.label: A
children.text: to ensure that the United States has a viable battery materials processing industry to supply the North American battery supply chain;

children.label: B
children.text: to expand the capabilities of the United States in advanced battery manufacturing;

children.label: C
children.text: to enhance national security by reducing the reliance of the United States on foreign competitors for critical materials and technologies; and

children.label: D
children.text: to enhance the domestic processing capacity of minerals necessary for battery materials and advanced batteries.

children.label: i
children.text: to carry out 1 or more demonstration projects in the United States for the processing of battery materials;

children.label: ii
children.text: to construct 1 or more new commercial-scale battery material processing facilities in the United States; and

children.label: iii
children.text: to retool, retrofit, or expand 1 or more existing battery material processing facilities located in the United States and determined qualified by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: $50,000,000 for an eligible entity carrying out 1 or more projects described in subparagraph (A)(i);

children.label: ii
children.text: $100,000,000 for an eligible entity carrying out 1 or more projects described in subparagraph (A)(ii); and

children.label: iii
children.text: $50,000,000 for an eligible entity carrying out 1 or more projects described in subparagraph (A)(iii).

children.label: I
children.text: is located and operates in the United States;

children.label: II
children.text: is owned by a United States entity;

children.label: III
children.text: deploys North American-owned intellectual property and content;

children.label: IV
children.text: represents consortia or industry partnerships; and

children.label: V
children.text: will not use battery material supplied by or originating from a foreign entity of concern; and

children.label: I
children.text: provides workforce opportunities in low- and moderate-income communities;

children.label: II
children.text: encourages partnership with universities and laboratories to spur innovation and drive down costs;

children.label: III
children.text: partners with Indian Tribes; and

children.label: aa
children.text: greenhouse gas emissions reductions and energy efficient battery material processing opportunities throughout the manufacturing process; and

children.label: bb
children.text: supply chain logistics.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 4 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out the program $3,000,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2022 through 2026, to remain available until expended.

children.heading: Battery manufacturing and recycling grants
children.label: c Battery manufacturing and recycling grants

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall establish within the Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy a battery manufacturing and recycling grant program (referred to in this subsection as the “program”).November 15, 2021

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: 2 Purpose
children.text: The purpose of the program is to ensure that the United States has a viable domestic manufacturing and recycling capability to support and sustain a North American battery supply chain.

children.label: i
children.text: to carry out 1 or more demonstration projects for advanced battery component manufacturing, advanced battery manufacturing, and recycling;

children.label: ii
children.text: to construct 1 or more new commercial-scale advanced battery component manufacturing, advanced battery manufacturing, or recycling facilities in the United States; and

children.label: iii
children.text: to retool, retrofit, or expand 1 or more existing facilities located in the United States and determined qualified by the Secretary for advanced battery component manufacturing, advanced battery manufacturing, and recycling.

children.label: i
children.text: $50,000,000 for an eligible entity carrying out 1 or more projects described in subparagraph (A)(i);

children.label: ii
children.text: $100,000,000 for an eligible entity carrying out 1 or more projects described in subparagraph (A)(ii); and

children.label: iii
children.text: $50,000,000 for an eligible entity carrying out 1 or more projects described in subparagraph (A)(iii).

children.label: I
children.text: is located and operates in the United States;

children.label: II
children.text: is owned by a United States entity;

children.label: III
children.text: deploys North American-owned intellectual property and content;

children.label: IV
children.text: represents consortia or industry partnerships; and

children.label: aa
children.text: if the eligible entity will use the grant for advanced battery component manufacturing, will not use battery material supplied by or originating from a foreign entity of concern; or

children.label: bb
children.text: if the eligible entity will use the grant for battery recycling, will not export recovered critical materials to a foreign entity of concern; and

children.label: I
children.text: provides workforce opportunities in low- and moderate-income or rural communities;

children.label: II
children.text: provides workforce opportunities in communities that have lost jobs due to the displacements of fossil energy jobs;

children.label: III
children.text: encourages partnership with universities and laboratories to spur innovation and drive down costs;

children.label: IV
children.text: partners with Indian Tribes;

children.label: aa
children.text: greenhouse gas emissions reductions and energy efficient battery material processing opportunities throughout the manufacturing process; and

children.label: bb
children.text: supply chain logistics; and

children.label: VI
children.text: utilizes feedstock produced in the United States.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 4 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out the program $3,000,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2022 through 2026, to remain available until expended.
the number of grant applications received;
the number of grants awarded and the amount of each award;
the purpose and status of each project carried out using a grant; and
any other information the Secretary determines necessary.

children.heading: Lithium-Ion Battery Recycling Prize Competition
children.label: e Lithium-Ion Battery Recycling Prize Competition

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall continue to carry out the Lithium-Ion Battery Recycling Prize Competition of the Department established pursuant to (referred to in this subsection as the “competition”).section 3719 of title 15

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations for pilot projects
children.label: 2 Authorization of appropriations for pilot projects

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out Phase III of the competition, $10,000,000 for fiscal year 2022, to remain available until expended.

children.label: i
children.text: to increase the number of winners of Phase III of the competition;

children.label: ii
children.text: to increase the amount awarded to each winner of Phase III of the competition; and

children.label: iii
children.text: to carry out any other activity that is consistent with the goals of Phase III of the competition, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Battery and critical mineral recycling
children.label: f Battery and critical mineral recycling

children.heading: Administrator
children.label: A Administrator
children.text: The term “Administrator” means the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency.

children.label: i
children.text: consists of 1 or more electrochemical cells that are electrically connected; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is designed to store and deliver electric energy.

children.label: I
children.text: manufactures the covered battery or covered battery-containing product; and

children.label: II
children.text: sells or offers for sale the covered battery or covered battery-containing product under the brand of that person.

children.label: ii
children.text: If there is no person described in clause (i) with respect to the covered battery or covered battery-containing product, the owner or licensee of the brand under which the covered battery or covered battery-containing product is sold, offered for sale, or distributed, regardless of whether the trademark of the brand is registered.

children.label: iii
children.text: If there is no person described in clause (i) or (ii) with respect to the covered battery or covered battery-containing product, a person that imports the covered battery or covered battery-containing product into the United States for sale or distribution.

children.heading: Covered battery
children.label: D Covered battery
children.text: The term “covered battery” means a new or unused primary battery or rechargeable battery.

children.heading: Covered battery-containing product
children.label: E Covered battery-containing product
children.text: The term “covered battery-containing product” means a new or unused product that contains or is packaged with a primary battery or rechargeable battery.

children.heading: Critical mineral
children.label: F Critical mineral
children.text: The term “critical mineral” has the meaning given the term in .section 1606(a) of title 30

children.heading: Primary battery
children.label: G Primary battery
children.text: The term “primary battery” means a nonrechargeable battery that weighs not more than 4.4 pounds, including an alkaline, carbon-zinc, and lithium metal battery.

children.label: I
children.text: contains 1 or more voltaic or galvanic cells that are electrically connected to produce electric energy;

children.label: II
children.text: is designed to be recharged;

children.label: III
children.text: weighs not more than 11 pounds; and

children.label: IV
children.text: has a watt-hour rating of not more than 300 watt-hours.

children.label: I
children.text: contains electrolyte as a free liquid; or

children.label: II
children.text: employs lead-acid technology, unless that battery is sealed and does not contain electrolyte as a free liquid.

children.label: i
children.text: during which recyclable materials are processed into specification-grade commodities, and consumed as raw-material feedstock, in lieu of virgin materials, in the manufacturing of new products;

children.label: ii
children.text: that may include collection, processing, and brokering; and

children.label: iii
children.text: that result in subsequent consumption by a materials manufacturer, including for the manufacturing of new products.

children.heading: Battery recycling research, development, and demonstration grants
children.label: 2 Battery recycling research, development, and demonstration grants
i
children.text: recycling activities;

children.label: ii
children.text: the development of methods to promote the design and production of batteries that take into full account and facilitate the dismantling, reuse, recovery, and recycling of battery components and materials;

children.label: iii
children.text: strategies to increase consumer acceptance of, and participation in, the recycling of batteries;

children.label: iv
children.text: the extraction or recovery of critical minerals from batteries that are recycled;

children.label: v
children.text: the integration of increased quantities of recycled critical minerals in batteries and other products to develop markets for recycled battery materials and critical minerals;

children.label: vi
children.text: safe disposal of waste materials and components recovered during the recycling process;

children.label: vii
children.text: the protection of the health and safety of all persons involved in, or in proximity to, recycling and reprocessing activities, including communities located near recycling and materials reprocessing facilities;

children.label: viii
children.text: mitigation of environmental impacts that arise from recycling batteries, including disposal of toxic reagents and byproducts related to recycling processes;

children.label: ix
children.text: protection of data privacy associated with collected covered battery-containing products;

children.label: x
children.text: the optimization of the value of material derived from recycling batteries; and

children.label: xi
children.text: the cost-effectiveness and benefits of the reuse and recycling of batteries and critical minerals.

children.label: i
children.text: an institution of higher education;

children.label: ii
children.text: a National Laboratory;

children.label: iii
children.text: a Federal research agency;

children.label: iv
children.text: a State research agency;

children.label: v
children.text: a nonprofit organization;

children.label: vi
children.text: an industrial entity;

children.label: vii
children.text: a manufacturing entity;

children.label: viii
children.text: a private battery-collection entity;

children.label: ix
children.text: an entity operating 1 or more battery recycling activities;

children.label: x
children.text: a State or municipal government entity;

children.label: xi
children.text: a battery producer;

children.label: xii
children.text: a battery retailer; or

children.label: xiii
children.text: a consortium of 2 or more entities described in clauses (i) through (xii).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant under subparagraph (A), an eligible entity described in subparagraph (B) shall submit to the Secretary an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary may require.

children.label: I
children.text: battery producers and manufacturers;

children.label: II
children.text: battery material and equipment manufacturers;

children.label: III
children.text: battery recyclers, collectors, and refiners; and

children.label: IV
children.text: retailers.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: D Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out this paragraph $60,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2022 through 2026.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary, in coordination with the Administrator, shall establish a program under which the Secretary shall award grants, on a competitive basis, to States and units of local government to assist in the establishment or enhancement of State battery collection, recycling, and reprocessing programs.

children.heading: Non-Federal cost share
children.label: B Non-Federal cost share
children.text: The non-Federal share of the cost of a project carried out using a grant under this paragraph shall be 50 percent of the cost of the project.

children.heading: Report
children.label: C Report
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , and annually thereafter, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report that describes the number of battery collection points established or enhanced, an estimate of jobs created, and the quantity of material collected as a result of the grants awarded under subparagraph (A).November 15, 2021

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: D Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out this paragraph $50,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2022 through 2026.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall award grants, on a competitive basis, to retailers that sell covered batteries or covered battery-containing products to establish and implement a system for the acceptance and collection of covered batteries and covered battery-containing products, as applicable, for reuse, recycling, or proper disposal.

children.label: i
children.text: at no cost to the consumer; and

children.label: ii
children.text: on a regular, convenient, and accessible basis.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: C Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out this paragraph $15,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2022 through 2026.

children.label: i
children.text: addresses battery recycling goals, cost structures for mandatory recycling, reporting requirements, product design, collection models, and transportation of collected materials;

children.label: ii
children.text: provides sufficient flexibility to allow battery producers to determine cost-effective strategies for compliance with the framework; and

children.label: iii
children.text: outlines regulatory pathways for effective recycling.

children.label: i
children.text: battery producers, manufacturers, retailers, recyclers, and collectors or processors;

children.label: ii
children.text: States and municipalities; and

children.label: iii
children.text: other relevant stakeholders, such as environmental, energy, or consumer organizations, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: describes the extended producer responsibility framework developed by the task force;

children.label: ii
children.text: includes the recommendations of the task force on how best to implement a mandatory pay-in or other enforcement mechanism to ensure that battery producers and sellers are contributing to the recycling of batteries; and

children.label: iii
children.text: suggests regulatory pathways for effective recycling.

children.heading: Effect on Mercury-Containing and Rechargeable Battery Management Act
children.label: 6 Effect on Mercury-Containing and Rechargeable Battery Management Act
children.text: Nothing in this subsection, or any regulation, guideline, framework, or policy adopted or promulgated pursuant to this subsection, shall modify or otherwise affect the provisions of the Mercury-Containing and Rechargeable Battery Management Act ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 14301
